content:
Character: Lucian Ardent
Perception: Lucian is almost unbearably beautiful — those grey-green eyes, the perfect jaw, clothes that cost more than my monthly rent. Seeing him is like swallowing glass; every inch of him reminds me of everything I'm not. Yet he sat across from me in the coffee shop, unprompted, looked at my screen, and said my lettering had "a kind of gravity." He commissioned a bookplate and paid triple without blinking. I can't decide if he's genuinely kind or just performing kindness effortlessly because the world has always been generous to him. He's my client and occasional coffee-shop acquaintance. I know his firm does something in finance, that he moved in recently, and that the scar on his eyebrow is the only honest thing about his face.
summary:

I can't decide if he's genuinely kind or just performing kindness effortlessly because the world has always been generous to him. He's my client and occasional coffee-shop acquaintance. I know his firm does something in finance, that he moved in recently, and that the scar on his eyebrow is the only honest thing about his face.
